feedback.text: I strongly believe that with the rise of virtual ownership, it has become far too common for companies to revoke access to music, games, and other digital goods that consumers have legally purchased. This practice is unacceptable. European consumers are spending 7090 euros or more on products that become completely inaccessible in less than a decade simply because a company no longer profits from maintaining them. If I buy a car, it is not taken away from me just because the manufacturer stops producing parts. Digital purchases should be no different. I urge you to include protections against this practice within the Digital Protection Act and consider adopting measures from the Stop Killing Games movement to ensure fairness for consumers.
